articles.title: Google Announces Angular v21
articles.description: The Google team has gone all out with this significant release of its popular JavaScript framework. They’ve put together a retro game-themed adventure-based tour of what’s new, along with top notch videos showing off features like its new signal-based approach to forms, MCP server for AI-powered workflows, library of headless components focused on accessibility, and even a new ‘Angular AI Tutor’ to get up to speed.
articles.author: Google

articles.title: Authentication. Complete, Not Complex
Tooltip Components Should Not Exist
articles.description: Dominik challenges some common wisdom in his typically erudite fashion. Stand-alone tooltip components are the wrong abstraction when separated from the underlying UI features that use them. This thinking can apply to many other UI affordances too, so the broad idea is well worth considering.
articles.author: Dominik Dorfmeister (AKA TkDodo)

articles.title: Implementing Wordle in LibreOffice with JavaScript Macros
articles.description: One of those fun things where the real value is in learning that you can script LibreOffice with JavaScript.
articles.author: Bojidar Marinov

articles.title: Automated NPM Secret Rotation in GitHub Actions
articles.description: If you’ve built a system to automatically publish to npm, you may have had to change things due to this month’s npm security changes. Here’s a way to keep things ticking if you’re not ready to switch to trusted publishing.
articles.author: Michael Heap

articles.title: Six Things I Bet You Didn't Know You Could Do With Chrome's DevTools
articles.description: Part one here covers the first three, with part two covering the last three. Covers time functions, DOM observation, user action replays, and more.
articles.author: Rachel Kaufman

articles.title: Webpack Bundle Analyzer 5.0: A Visual Look at Webpack's Output
articles.description: An official Webpack plugin and CLI tool that shows bundle content in the form of an interactive zoomable treemap so you can dig in and see what’s taking up space in your bundle (and then optimize it, ideally).
articles.author: Webpack Project

articles.title: TanStack DB 0.5, Now with Query-Driven Sync
articles.description: TanStack DB is a client‑first reactive data store that uses differential dataflow to power live, relational queries, sub‑ms incremental updates, and seamless optimistic writes. In v0.5, a component’s query becomes the API call too. “Just write your query and TanStack DB figures out exactly what to fetch.”
articles.author: Willis, De Parre, and Matthews

articles.title: Brimstone: A New JavaScript Engine Written in Rust
articles.description: Joining the hundreds of JS engines available, Brimstone has strong language support (97% of the spec), is very small, but remains a work in progress.
articles.author: Hans Halverson

articles.title: VueFinder: File Management Component for Vue 3
articles.description: Provides a reactive, native-like file explorer interface for users to organize, preview, and manage files.
articles.author: Yusuf Özdemir

articles.title: is-online 12.0: Check if the Internet Connection Is Up
articles.description: Works in both Node and the browser and uses various approaches to check if the Internet is really available.
articles.author: Sindre Sorhus

articles.description: Devographics' annual State of React survey is now open to take again. Here's last year's results.
🎤 TypeScript's Daniel Rosenwasser and Jake Bailey went on the TypeScript.fm podcast to talk about what's coming up in TypeScript 6 and 7.
📗 TC-39 member James M. Snell is working on a book called JavaScript in Depth for Manning. Final publication is expected in mid 2026 but four chapters are already available in early access.
CSS Grid support should be coming to React Native soon.
Prisma 7.0 – Popular ORM for Node.js and TypeScript. The Rust-free Prisma Client is now the default.
pnpm 10.23 – Fast, space efficient package manager.
Node.js v25.2.1 (Current), Astro 5.16
Iterator Sequencing progressed to stage 4.
Joint Iteration, Iterator Join, and Await dictionary of Promises go stage 2.7.
Error.captureStackTrace, import .. with {type: "text"}, and Object.keysLength all progressed to stage 2.
The Intl Unit Protocol also reached stage 1 to provide a way to annotate quantities with the units being measured.
Typed Array Find Within progressed to stage 1. Think a native indexOf-type method for TypedArrays.

articles.title: The Cloudflare Outage of November 18, 2025
articles.description: Yesterday, as I’m sure many of you are aware, Cloudflare’s network saw a significant hours-long failure, resulting in widespread errors and access issues. Here's a detailed post-mortem, explaining what happened — pointing to a database permissions change as the culprit. The outage only highlights how Cloudflare, along with AWS, serve as critical parts of the overall infrastructure of the modern web.
articles.author: Matthew Prince

articles.title: Six Things I Bet You Didn't Know You Could Do With Chrome's Devtools
articles.description: Part one here covers the first three, with part two covering the last three. Covers time functions, DOM observation, user action replays, and more.
articles.author: Rachel Kaufman

articles.title: The Range Syntax Has Come to Container Style Queries and if()
articles.description: Range syntax is now available as of Chrome 142 — as such, here’s an explainer of how it can be used with practical examples demonstrating it with custom properties, the attr() function, and literal values.
articles.author: Daniel Schwarz

articles.title: The Story of a Font Licencing 'Shakedown'
articles.description: A cautionary yet enganging tale recounting a dubious licensing claim from a major font publisher, based on automated scans, that was ultimately inaccurate. Yet, there are surely companies who would fold without such due diligence on display here.
articles.author: Ameel Khan

articles.title: When Everyone’s a Developer, How Do We Promote the Web Platform Over React?
articles.description: A thought piece from the former founder of ReadWriteWeb on the idea that AI models ‘default to React and Next.js’ in preference to using widely available web/browser-native solutions.
articles.author: Richard MacManus

articles.title: Older Tech in the Browser Stack
articles.description: There’s plenty of existing web features and technologies that you may not be aware of, or never touch directly in your day-to-day work. Bryan looks at one such example here with XPath, demonstrating how it can be used alongside CSS to query elements.
articles.author: Bryan Rasmussen

articles.title: Enhancing Web Components Safely with Self-Destructing CSS
articles.description: Shares a way to reveal a hidden web component without relying on JavaScript.
articles.author: Scott Jehl

articles.title: Firefox to Add Support for a Feature Users First Requested in 2004
articles.description: Namely, customizable keyboard shortcuts.
articles.author: Martin Brinkmann

articles.title: css-extras: Useful CSS Custom Functions using the New @function Rule
articles.description: The @function at-rule is still not widely available, but you might want to use this repository of 40+ custom functions to get a feel for the syntax, and maybe find something to use as browser support improves.
articles.author: Sindre Sorhus

articles.title: JS Game Rendering Benchmark: A Sprite-Based Performance Test to Compare JavaScript Game Libraries
articles.description: Concludes that Babylon.js, Pixi.js, and Phaser are the best performing libraries with some honorable mentions. You can view and customize the test yourself here.
articles.author: Jonny Ngo Luong

articles.title: ESLint Plugin for Baseline JavaScript
articles.description: This is the winner of a recent 'Baseline Tooling Hackathon'. It's an ESLint plugin that flags code using features newer than your configured Baseline target.
articles.author: Ryuya Hasegawa

articles.title: Baseline Status for Video: Easily Show Baseline Status
articles.description: A niche tool that may come in handy for those creating dev videos. It allows you to create a short customizable video clip (to insert in your video) that displays MDN’s Baseline Status for a particular CSS feature.
articles.author: Zoran Jambor

articles.title: ImageConverter.dev: A 100% Local, Web-Based Image Converter
articles.description: Includes support for 9 different image formats with a quality setting for output. There are also a few sister sites: an image resizer, bulk resizer, and a website screenshot tool.
articles.author: Crownbyte
